---
title: "03_analisisdatosmasivos"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Quarto

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Running Code

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)


```

You can add options to executable code like this

```{r}
#| echo: false
datos <- read_excel("/Users/alejandrocamposlamas/Proyectos/ACTIVIDADES_UNIR/Cuatrimestre_2/Analisis_Datos_Masivos_2/datos/cpatrones_starbucks.xlsx")
```

```{r}
#dim(datos)

colnames(datos)
str(datos)
colSums(is.na(datos))
summary(datos)



```

```{r }
# queremos predecir cuanto se van a gastar
library(dplyr)

datos_regresion <- datos%>%select("day_of_week", "order_channel", "store_location_type" , "region", "customer_age_group", "customer_gender","customer_gender","is_rewards_member","cart_size","num_customizations" ,"total_spend","fulfillment_time_min","drink_category","has_food_item" ,"order_ahead","customer_satisfaction" )

datos_regresion$day_of_week<- as.factor(datos_regresion$day_of_week)

datos_regresion$order_channel<- as.factor(datos_regresion$order_channel)

datos_regresion$store_location_type<- as.factor(datos_regresion$store_location_type)

datos_regresion$region<- as.factor(datos_regresion$region)

datos_regresion$customer_age_group<- as.factor(datos_regresion$customer_age_group)

datos_regresion$customer_gender<- as.factor(datos_regresion$customer_gender)

datos_regresion$is_rewards_member<- as.factor(datos_regresion$is_rewards_member)

datos_regresion$drink_category<- as.factor(datos_regresion$drink_category)

datos_regresion$has_food_item<- as.factor(datos_regresion$has_food_item)

datos_regresion$order_ahead<- as.factor(datos_regresion$order_ahead)



```

```{r reproducibilidad}
set.seed(123)

indice <- sample(1:nrow(datos_regresion), size=0.8*nrow(datos_regresion))

train <- datos_regresion[indice, ]
test <- datos_regresion[-indice, ]


modelo_reg_lineal <- lm(total_spend~day_of_week+customer_age_group+num_customizations+has_food_item+order_channel+customer_gender +order_ahead+store_location_type+is_rewards_member+fulfillment_time_min+customer_satisfaction+region+cart_size+drink_category, data=train)



```

```{r}
summary(modelo_reg_lineal)
```

```{r}

predicciones <- predict(modelo_reg_lineal, newdata=test)

```

```{r}
mse <- mean((test$total_spend-predicciones)^2)
rmse <- sqrt(mse)

mse
rmse
```
